hero_subtitle: SFTY helps regulatory teams translate complex jurisdictional frameworks into structured supervisory workflows with evidentiary trails and integrated oversight controls.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Regulatory frameworks remain disconnected from workflows
problem_description: Government teams face challenges translating complex jurisdiction-specific requirements into actionable supervisory processes. This gap slows regulatory review readiness and makes it difficult to maintain consistent, auditable policy enforcement across digital asset markets.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY aligns jurisdiction-specific regulatory frameworks with supervisory actions by connecting legal policies to case workflows, evidence trails, and reporting processes within one auditable operating layer for regulators.
workflow_title: From regulatory update to internal rules
workflow_description: SFTY maps jurisdictional policies and regulatory updates into tailored internal rules that guide supervisory workflows, case management, and compliance review.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. Supervisory posture unified
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented regulatory data, policy frameworks and market signals into one evidence-ready layer for structured supervisory workflows and jurisdictional oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY delivers a secure, auditable platform for public authorities requiring evidence-grade logging, strict access control and procurement-safe deployment in regulated environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how policy mapping connects to supervisory workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how regulatory policies translate into compliance cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports policy alignment reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for jurisdictional policy frameworks
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects regulatory data, VASP and CASP profiles, licensing records, wallets, transactions, cases and reports into one secure supervisory layer for regulators.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support policy mapping workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY links legal requirements to workflows by mapping jurisdictional policies onto supervision, enabling risk-based alerts, case creation and structured reporting workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and evidence-grade audit records to support review readiness and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: How is SFTY deployed in government environments?
faq_4_answer: SFTY supports flexible deployment options including on-premise and private cloud, with role-based access, audit logging and controlled governance.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing regulatory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing analytics and compliance tools, integrating multiple provider outputs without replacing them.
final_title: Align supervisory workflows with policy mapping
final_description: Review how SFTY connects regulatory frameworks to structured supervisory cases, evidence records and government deployment workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
